page: Amazon Web Services r8i.96xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor and x86_64 architecture. It features dedicated Intel Xeon 6975P-C processors with 192 physical cores and 384 vCPUs running at 3.9 GHz. The processor configuration includes a 480 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for vector and matrix math operations. The system is equipped with 3072.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 7 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per physical core. Local storage is not bundled with this instance, and network connectivity is established with a 100 Gbps baseline bandwidth.

In benchmark evaluations, the r8i.96xlarge demonstrates top-tier multi-core CPU performance, placing in the top 10% of tested systems. Its memory subsystem achieves top-tier read and write bandwidth, with uncached large-block reads reaching 1125.32 GB/sec. Database and in-memory operations are highly efficient, as evidenced by top-tier results in Redis SET operations and Passmark database tests. While the instance lacks dedicated GPU hardware, its Intel AMX capabilities support strong prompt processing speeds for large language models, though text generation speeds remain average to poor compared to GPU-accelerated alternatives.

Given its massive memory footprint and high vCPU count, the r8i.96xlarge offers high resource density for enterprise-scale workloads. The absence of local NVMe storage requires reliance on network-attached storage, which is supported by the 100 Gbps network interface. This profile is highly cost-efficient for memory-bound applications that do not require local scratch space but demand massive RAM capacity and high-throughput memory access. It is ideally suited for large-scale in-memory databases, high-performance computing simulations, big data analytics, and enterprise resource planning systems.
description: Amazon Web Services r8i.96xlarge is a memory-optimized server powered by the Nitro hypervisor and Intel Xeon 6975P-C processors. Operating on the x86_64 architecture, it delivers 384 vCPUs across 192 physical cores at 3.9 GHz, supported by 3072.0 GB of high-speed 7 GHz DDR5 memory. The instance lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs, relying instead on a 100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmarks show top-tier multi-core CPU performance and exceptional memory bandwidth, alongside top-tier results in Redis and database operations. While its lack of a GPU limits text generation speeds, its Intel AMX instructions enable strong prompt processing. This instance is highly suited for memory-intensive workloads, including large-scale in-memory databases, enterprise applications, and data-heavy analytical processing.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r8i.96xlarge is a memory-optimized server featuring 384 vCPUs, 3072 GB of DDR5 RAM, and 100 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier database and multi-core performance.

tagline: A high-density memory-optimized platform featuring massive DDR5 bandwidth and dedicated Intel Xeon processors for enterprise database workloads.
bullet_points: 384 vCPUs and 192 physical cores powered by Intel Xeon 6975P-C processors.
3072.0 GB of high-speed 7 GHz DDR5 memory for memory-intensive applications.
Top-tier multi-core CPU performance and memory read/write bandwidth.
100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no bundled local storage.
Intel AMX support for accelerated matrix operations and prompt processing.
Optimized for large-scale in-memory databases, ERP systems, and data analytics.
